provenance.source_corpus: Techniques for restoring fen vegetation on cut‐away peatlands in North America

source_spans.text: Sphagnum transfer, a reintroduction technique commonly used for bog restoration in North America, was effective for establishing Sphagnum and Carex species.
The Sphagnum transfer was a superior method for reintroducing fen vegetation, as the covers of Sphagnum, Carex and total vegetation were higher on these experimental units
The treatments which included light phosphorus fertilization, had a higher Carex cover after three growing seasons.
The Sphagnum transfer was a superior method for reintroducing fen vegetation, as the covers of Sphagnum, Carex and total vegetation were higher on these experimental units
The treatments which included light phosphorus fertilization, had a higher Carex cover after three growing seasons.
Moreover, species richness was significantly higher where vegetation had been reintroduced (24 ± 3 species per unit) than where vegetation had not been reintroduced (22 ± 2 species per unit)
Moreover, species richness was significantly higher where vegetation had been reintroduced (24 ± 3 species per unit) than where vegetation had not been reintroduced (22 ± 2 species per unit)
The timing of the reintroductions had no impact on the success of vegetation establishment.
The timing of the reintroductions had no impact on the success of vegetation establishment.
